color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">Genomic Medicine in Africa</span></div>" style="text;html=1;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;whiteSpace=wrap;rounded=0;fontSize=70;fontFamily=Fira Sans Light;fontStyle=1" vertex="1" parent="19zFNY2nmE2F9cugnkCF-1"> <mxGeometry x="150" y="5" width="1450" height="140" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="19zFNY2nmE2F9cugnkCF-3" value="<span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">Africa remains underrepresented in global genomic research despite its rich genetic diversity, which holds crucial insights for understanding disease mechanisms and improving precision medicine.&nbsp;</span><span><br></span><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><br></span><div><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">The lack of computationally efficient and context-aware bioinformatics workflows hinders the variant interpretation and clinical translation.&nbsp;</span><span><br><br></span><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));">This project will contribute to capacity building in genomic data analysis and strengthen bioinformatics expertise and infrastructure for Genomic Medicine in Africa.</span><span></span></div>" style="text;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;whiteSpace=wrap;rounded=0;fontSize=40;fontFamily=Fira Sans Light;fontStyle=0" vertex="1" parent="19zFNY2nmE2F9cugnkCF-1"> <mxGeometry x="30" y="160" width="1500" height="506" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="DVsmABH0-CBWBcZBED-O-2" value="" style="shape=image;verticalLabelPosition=bottom;labelBackgroundColor=default;verticalAlign=top;aspect=fixed;imageAspect=0;image=data:image/gif,;fontFamily=Fira Sans Light;" vertex="1" parent="19zFNY2nmE2F9cugnkCF-1"> <mxGeometry width="150" height="150" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="oRxT3crQ0uBmT7DFYTrY-0" value="<div><b style="font-style: normal;">Key Reading:</b></div>Pedersen, B. div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 1448px; height: 1px; padding-top: 75px; margin-left: 152px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;

display: inline-block; font-size: 70px; font-family: "Fira Sans Light"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-weight: bold; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.div: Relevance to Genomic Medicine in Africa

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1498px; height: 1px; padding-top: 413px; margin-left: 31px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 40px; font-family: "Fira Sans Light"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
div.span: Africa remains underrepresented in global genomic research despite its rich genetic diversity, which holds crucial insights for understanding disease mechanisms and improving precision medicine.
The lack of computationally efficient and context-aware bioinformatics workflows hinders the variant interpretation and clinical translation.
This project will contribute to capacity building in genomic data analysis and strengthen bioinformatics expertise and infrastructure for Genomic Medicine in Africa.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1498px; height: 1px; padding-top: 770px; margin-left: 51px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;

display: inline-block; font-size: 35px; font-family: "Fira Sans Light"; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; font-style: italic; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div: Key Reading:Pedersen, B. S. & Quinlan, A. R. (2021). Effective variant filtering and expected candidate counts for Mendelian disease studies. Genome Medicine / PMC
